final_rules.instructions: This rule requires the WAF engine to perform full normalization first (e.g., lowercase, urlDecodeUni, htmlEntityDecode, jsDecode, cssDecode). It is designed to catch common XSS vector tags after any encoding or obfuscation has been removed by the engine's transformation functions. It is a high-confidence indicator when matched on normalized input.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Optimized the regex by consolidating tags into a non-capturing group `(?:...)`. Expanded the list of tags to include other HTML5 vectors like `video`, `audio`, `details`, `object`, and `embed`. This rule is a more precise replacement for the overly broad existing rules `id:1302` (`<`) and `id:1303` (`>`).
final_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?i)<\s*/?\s*(?:script|svg|img|iframe|body|video|audio|form|object|embed|details)\b" "msg:XSS: Potentially malicious HTML tag" "mz:ARGS|BODY|URL|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:6" id:920420;

final_rules.instructions: This rule also requires full normalization by the WAF engine before matching. It detects not only standard 'on*' event handlers but also other attributes that can be abused to execute scripts or create XSS conditions, such as `formaction` or `srcdoc`. The word boundary `\b` ensures accuracy, preventing matches on substrings like 'version=...'.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Refined the original pattern `(?:^|[^a-z0-9_:-])on...` to use a more performant and accurate word boundary `\b`. Broadened coverage by adding other attributes known to be abusable for XSS (`style`, `background`, `srcdoc`, `formaction`).
final_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?i)\b(on[a-z]{3,24}|formaction|style|background|srcdoc)\s*=" "msg:XSS: HTML event handler or dangerous attribute" "mz:ARGS|BODY|URL|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:7" id:920421;

final_rules.instructions: A behavioral rule that detects common JavaScript functions and properties used in XSS proof-of-concept payloads and attacks. Requires full WAF normalization to be effective against obfuscated payloads. This rule acts as a strong secondary indicator of malicious intent.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Expanded the list of sensitive JavaScript functions and DOM sinks to include more dangerous methods like `eval`, `String.fromCharCode`, `document.write`, and various `location` manipulations, providing broader coverage against script execution techniques.
final_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?i)(?:alert|prompt|confirm|eval|atob|String\.fromCharCode)\s*(?:\(|`)|(?:document\.(?:cookie|domain|write)|location\.(?:href|assign|replace)|window\.open)" "msg:XSS: JavaScript execution sink or sensitive DOM access" "mz:ARGS|BODY|URL|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:5" id:920422;

final_rules.instructions: CRITICAL: This rule must be configured to run on the RAW, un-normalized input values. It is an anomaly detection rule specifically designed to catch the Unicode and full-width character evasions seen in the bypassed payloads. Due to its nature, it may cause false positives on applications that legitimately use CJK or other non-ASCII languages in free-text fields. If false positives occur, scope this rule to URL and specific ARGS known to not contain such data.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Simplified and hardened the original regex (`id:140010`). The pattern `[пјҒ-пҪһ]{6,}` covers the entire range of full-width ASCII-mappable characters, making it more generic and future-proof than the original hardcoded list. This rule directly addresses the primary evasion vector.
final_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:[^\x00-\x7f]{6,}|[пјҒ-пҪһ]{6,}" "msg:XSS: Anomaly - High-density of non-ASCII or full-width characters" "mz:ARGS|BODY|URL" "s:$XSS:5" id:920423;

final_rules.instructions: This is an anomaly detection rule that must run on RAW, un-normalized input. It detects various signs of intentional evasion, including deprecated IIS encoding (`%u...`), double URL-encoding (`%25..`), and long, contiguous sequences of mixed encoding types. It acts as an early warning for attempts to bypass WAF normalization.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Consolidated and improved upon rules `id:140007` and existing `id:1315`. The new regex is more precise, looking for specific high-confidence patterns like `%u` and `%25` or a sequence of 5 or more encoded entities to significantly reduce the false positives that the original `(?:%[0-9a-f]{2}){3,}` pattern would have generated.
final_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?:%u[0-9a-f]{4}|%25[0-9a-f]{2})|(?:(?:&#x?[0-9a-f]{2,4};|%[0-9a-f]{2}|\\x[0-9a-f]{2}){5,})" "msg:XSS: Anomaly - Excessive or layered encoding detected" "mz:ARGS|BODY|URL" "s:$XSS:4" id:920424;

generated_rules.instructions: Primary high-confidence XSS rule. Naxsi already decodes url-encoded and hexadecimal sequences before matching, so this covers many single and multi-layer encoded payloads after upstream decoding. Apply globally. Recommended action: BLOCK via CheckRule at low threshold.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?i)(?:<(?:script|svg|img|iframe|body)\b|(?:^|[^a-z0-9_:-])on(?:load|error|click|mouseover|focus|start|readystatechange|animationstart|begin)\s*=|(?:src|href|data|action|formaction)\s*=\s*['\"`]?\s*(?:javascript|vbscript)\s*:|alert\s*\(|prompt\s*\(|confirm\s*\()" "msg:XSS core tag event and script scheme detection on decoded input" "mz:ARGS|BODY|URL|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:8" id:140001;
generated_rules.validation_error: Invalid regex pattern: bad escape (end of pattern) at position 190

generated_rules.instructions: Catches direct tag injection attempts, including mixed spacing. Use alongside the core rule to score partial payloads. Recommended action: BLOCK when combined with other indicators; LOG if isolated in content-heavy applications until tuned.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?i)<\s*/?\s*script\b|<\s*/?\s*svg\b|<\s*img\b|<\s*iframe\b|<\s*body\b" "msg:HTML tag injection markers commonly used in XSS payloads" "mz:ARGS|BODY|URL|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:6" id:140002;

generated_rules.instructions: Generalized event-handler detection for obfuscated forms such as onload, onerror, onclick. Intended to catch variants not explicitly enumerated. Tune with whitelists for applications that legitimately submit raw HTML. Recommended action: BLOCK.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?i)(?:^|[^a-z0-9_:-])on[a-z]{3,24}\s*=\s*[^=]" "msg:Inline event handler attribute pattern" "mz:ARGS|BODY|URL|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:7" id:140003;

generated_rules.instructions: Targets javascript: and vbscript: URI execution vectors, including iframe/src and similar assignments. High confidence for reflected and stored XSS payloads. Recommended action: BLOCK.
generated_rules.validation_error: Invalid regex pattern: bad escape (end of pattern) at position 48

generated_rules.instructions: Behavioral indicator for execution intent. Useful against payload mutations where tag syntax varies but execution primitive remains. Recommended action: CHALLENGE or BLOCK when score threshold is met with another rule.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?i)(?:alert|prompt|confirm|settimeout|setinterval)\s*(?:\(|`)|(?:document\.(?:cookie|domain|location))" "msg:JavaScript execution primitive or sensitive DOM sink" "mz:ARGS|BODY|URL|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:5" id:140005;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects HTML entity based tag construction such as <svg. This helps cover entity-obfuscated payloads. Recommended action: LOG alone, BLOCK when combined with tag/event/script indicators.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?i)(?:&#(?:x0{0,4}3c|0*60);|&#(?:x0{0,4}3e|0*62);|&(?:lt|gt|quot|#x27|#39);)" "msg:Encoded HTML metacharacters used for tag construction" "mz:ARGS|BODY|URL|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:4" id:140006;

generated_rules.instructions: Anomaly rule for multi-layer encoding and heavy obfuscation. Naxsi decodes encoded sequences, but this still flags suspicious over-encoding patterns present in raw request components. Recommended action: LOG or CHALLENGE by itself; BLOCK when combined with XSS semantics.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?i)(?:%25){1,}|(?:%[0-9a-f]{2}){3,}|(?:&#[0-9]{2,5};){2,}|(?:&#x[0-9a-f]{2,4};){2,}" "msg:Excessive layered encoding or entity obfuscation" "mz:ARGS|BODY|URL|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:4" id:140007;

generated_rules.instructions: Catches common obfuscation methods used to split script tokens or hide payload components. Useful as a scoring amplifier rather than a standalone blocker. Recommended action: LOG, contribute to BLOCK through cumulative score.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?i)(?:/\*.*?\*/|<!--[\s\S]*?-->|\\x[0-9a-f]{2}|\\u[0-9a-f]{4})" "msg:Comment or escape-sequence obfuscation pattern" "mz:ARGS|BODY|URL|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:3" id:140008;

generated_rules.instructions: Purpose-built high-confidence signatures for the exact attack families seen: svg/onload, img/onerror, iframe/src=javascript:, and script tags. Recommended action: BLOCK immediately at threshold.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?i)(?:<\s*svg\b[^>]{0,256}\bon[a-z]{3,24}\s*=|<\s*img\b[^>]{0,256}\bonerror\s*=|<\s*iframe\b[^>]{0,256}\bsrc\s*=\s*['\"`]?\s*(?:javascript|vbscript)\s*:|<\s*script\b[^>]{0,256}>)" "msg:High-confidence executable XSS tag constructions" "mz:ARGS|BODY|URL|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:9" id:140009;

generated_rules.instructions: Anomaly detector for payloads dominated by fullwidth or non-ASCII confusable characters, as seen in the bypass samples. Keep score modest to reduce false positives on multilingual traffic. If your application legitimately handles CJK-heavy free text, restrict this rule to URL and selected ARGS instead of BODY. Recommended action: LOG or CHALLENGE unless combined with XSS indicators.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?i)(?:[^\x00-\x7f]{6,}|[пј…пјғпјҶпјҸпјқпјЎ-пјәпҪҒ-пҪҡпјҗ-пјҷ]{8,})" "msg:Unicode confusable or fullwidth-heavy input anomaly near attack surface" "mz:ARGS|BODY|URL|HEADERS" "s:$RFI:0,$XSS:3" id:140010;

generated_rules.instructions: Primary enforcement threshold. High-confidence single-rule hits or multiple medium-confidence hits are blocked.
Logging threshold for tuning and incident visibility. Review matched IDs and add BasicRule whitelists for known-safe parameters if needed.

Optional template whitelist only if a specific application endpoint legitimately accepts raw inline event-handler-like strings. Do not enable globally; scope to exact location/parameter in your NGINX location context.
